derivation: Selectors are keccak256 of the canonical signatures of the DEPLOYED COW Shed v1.0.x generation: the cow-shed sources at the v1.0.1 tag, cross-checked against the deployed-runtime factory ABI shipped by the TypeScript arbiter. The contract test re-derives every selector with an independent keccak implementation (sha3 crate) and asserts the macro-emitted SolCall::SELECTOR constant equals the pinned value for every bound function, so the fixture, the keccak preimages, and the sol! bindings must agree three ways. Deliberately out of scope: the factory's inherited ENS resolver read surface (initializeEns, addr, name, baseName, baseNode, resolution-node getters, supportsInterface) and the constructor-only NoCodeAtImplementation error; the v2.x-only pre-sign family exists in no deployed v1.0.x contract and is not bound.

factory_methods.name: executeHooks((address,uint256,bytes,bool,bool)[],bytes32,uint256,address,bytes)
factory_methods.rationale: Primary hook-execution entry point: deploys the user proxy if needed and forwards to the proxy's executeHooks

factory_methods.name: initializeProxy(address,bool)
factory_methods.rationale: Canonical 2-arg deployed form (withEns flag); the ENS-purged 1-arg form exists only in the unsupported v2.x generation

factory_methods.name: proxyOf(address)
factory_methods.rationale: On-chain CREATE2 derivation the SDK's proxy_of/proxy_for mirror byte-for-byte

factory_methods.name: implementation()
factory_methods.rationale: Immutable implementation getter participating in the CREATE2 init-code hash

shed_methods.name: executeHooks((address,uint256,bytes,bool,bool)[],bytes32,uint256,bytes)
shed_methods.rationale: Proxy-side hook execution (owner implicit); the factory entry point forwards here

shed_methods.name: trustedExecuteHooks((address,uint256,bytes,bool,bool)[])
shed_methods.rationale: Trusted-executor execution path without signature verification

shed_methods.name: claimWithResolver(address)
shed_methods.rationale: Reverse-resolution resolver claim for ENS opt-outs

shed_methods.name: updateTrustedExecutor(address)
shed_methods.rationale: Admin rotation of the trusted executor

shed_methods.name: updateImplementation(address)
shed_methods.rationale: Admin upgrade of the proxy implementation

shed_methods.name: revokeNonce(bytes32)
shed_methods.rationale: Admin revocation of an unused hook nonce

shed_methods.name: nonces(bytes32)
shed_methods.rationale: Nonce-consumption read used for replay checks

shed_methods.name: domainSeparator()
shed_methods.rationale: Per-proxy EIP-712 domain separator the SDK recomputes off-chain

shed_methods.name: VERSION()
shed_methods.rationale: Deployed version string; doubles as the EIP-712 domain version mirrored by CowShedVersion::version_str

shed_methods.name: initialize(address,bool)
shed_methods.rationale: Deployed 2-arg init (factory, claimResolver); this selector is embedded in both proxy creation-code blobs as the pre-initialization call guard

type_strings.Call: Call(address target,uint256 value,bytes callData,bool allowFailure,bool isDelegateCall)
type_strings.ExecuteHooks: ExecuteHooks(Call[] calls,bytes32 nonce,uint256 deadline)Call(address target,uint256 value,bytes callData,bool allowFailure,bool isDelegateCall)

signature_byte_order.rationale: decodeEOASignature requires exactly 65 bytes and reads r, then s, then v; the ERC-2098 compact round-trip is pinned by cow_shed/eoa_signature_byte_order.json against RecoverableSignature
